§ Articles

Engineering Knowledge

Vibration Analysis
FFT-Based Vibration Analysis for Rotating Machinery
How to use Fast Fourier Transform to diagnose bearing faults, unbalance and misalignment in industrial machinery — a practical guide with MATLAB examples.
Coming soon~12 min read
Power Transmission
How to Calculate V-Belt Drive Systems from Scratch
Step-by-step methodology for selecting belt cross-section, calculating drive ratio, centre distance, belt length and verifying power capacity per ISO 22.
Coming soon~10 min read
Structural
Beam Deflection: Theory, Formulas and Worked Examples
Euler-Bernoulli beam theory explained clearly. Deflection formulas for simply supported, cantilever and propped beams with step-by-step examples.
Coming soon~15 min read
Manufacturing
GD&T Basics: Tolerancing for the Practical Engineer
Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing fundamentals — datums, form tolerances, orientation and position. What you actually need to know on the shop floor.
Coming soon~14 min read
Materials
Choosing Between S235 and S355: When Does It Matter?
A practical comparison of the two most common structural steels — yield strength, weldability, cost difference and when to specify which grade.
Coming soon~8 min read
Thermodynamics
Heat Transfer Through Composite Walls: Worked Examples
Thermal resistance networks for flat and cylindrical walls. Calculating heat flux, surface temperatures and overall U-value for industrial insulation problems.
Coming soon~11 min read

§ About

6NGen Studio

6NGen is a one-person engineering studio based in Istanbul. The work sits at an unusual intersection: mechanical engineering knowledge, software development and agricultural experience — all brought together in tools that are actually useful in the field.

The studio makes two kinds of things: engineering calculators and reference tools for the technical community, and offline-first apps for farmers and beekeepers who need reliable software without subscriptions or internet dependency.

Every tool is built and tested by the same person who designs it. There are no product managers or user research departments — just an engineer who uses what he makes, and who cares whether it works correctly.

The name 6NGen comes from six degrees of freedom in mechanical systems — a nod to the kind of thinking that underpins all the work here: systematic, multi-axis, grounded in physical reality.

FounderÖmer Faruk Durna
EducationIstanbul Univ.
Mechanical Eng.
